all_findings.narration: An initial credit of ₹9000.00 was made to account acct_001 on 2024-04-11 from GORRE MADHUSUDHAN via UPI-CR-449934896313, which was then traced into 23 subsequent debits, with the largest debit being ₹1001.00 to Rajesh Aditya Kapoor on 2024-04-11 via UPI-DR-769341178950. The tracing of this credit into subsequent debits indicates a potential money trail, with ₹7794.00 successfully allocated and ₹1206.00 remaining untraced, suggesting that the funds were primarily used for various transactions including payments to individuals such as Menthi Hemalatha, SARVANJI HIMTA RAM, and PASUPULETI SUMAN, as well as to businesses like APSRTC and Bharti Airtel Limited, highlighting the complexity of the money trail and the need for further investigation to understand the purpose and legitimacy of these transactions.

edge_policy.description: Clusters are built from an evidence-protected reconstruction graph. Raw graph edges remain available for audit, but weak public-infrastructure or boundary edges do not merge accounts into one case cluster.
rules: Always keep edges that support a fired detector finding.
Keep low-degree non-account endpoints so mule UPI/cash-out destinations are not dropped.
Do not let high-degree public endpoints merge case clusters unless a fired detector already uses that edge.

cluster_summaries.summary: cluster_001 contains 1 connected account(s) and 1 transaction edge(s). The cluster evidence includes failed_reversed_transaction_detection, money_trail_tracing, reversal_clusters, round_value_debit_patterns.

case_summary: The case contains 1 account cluster(s), with 1 account(s) ranked for suspicious activity. The highest-priority cluster is cluster_001 with total score 404.80.
